sanskrit: नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना । न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥
hindiTranslation: जिसके मनइन्द्रियाँ संयमित नहीं हैं, ऐसे मनुष्यकी व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि नहीं होती और व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि न होनेसे उसमें कर्तव्यपरायणताकी भावना नहीं होती ।  ऐसी भावना न होनेसे उसको शान्ति नहीं मिलती ।  फिर शान्तिरहित मनुष्यको सुख कैसे मिल सकता है ॥
englishTranslation: A restless mind has no steady discernment, no steady purpose, no peace, and without peace, no happiness.
essence: Without inner discipline, even happiness has no place to stand.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who cannot control the mind and senses lacks clear judgment, steady purpose, peace, and therefore real happiness.
simpleInsight: Peace is not accidental; it grows from inner discipline.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna explains that a mind ruled by the senses cannot hold steady judgment or purpose. Without that inner order, peace cannot arise, and without peace, happiness slips away.
modernRelevance: You open your phone to calm down, then ten minutes later you are more scattered than before. Krishna points to the real problem: a mind that cannot hold itself together cannot produce peace on demand.
translationLiteral: नास्ति (there is not) / बुद्धिः (steady discernment) / अयुक्तस्य (of one who is unrestrained) / न (not) / च (and) / अयुक्तस्य (of one who is unrestrained) / भावना (steady purpose) / न (not) / च (and) / अभावयतः (of one who lacks inner cultivation) / शान्तिः (peace) / अशान्तस्य (of one without peace) / कुतः (how can there be) / सुखम् (happiness)
insight: It shifts attention from chasing comfort to building inner steadiness first.

quoteVariants.text: No restraint, no steady mind. No peace, no happiness.
Without inner order, peace never arrives.
